movement.title: Movement
movement.body:
Move between rooms with directional commands:
  n/s/e/w           - cardinal directions
  ne/nw/se/sw       - diagonals
  u/d               - up and down
  go <dir>          - explicit form, e.g. 'go north'

Use 'look' (or 'l') to redescribe the room.
Use 'brief' or 'verbose' to control how much description you see.

look.title: look
look.body:
Usage: look
       l

Displays the current room's name, description (if verbose), exits, items on the floor, monsters, and other players.

brief.title: brief / verbose
brief.body:
Usage: brief        - show room name + exits only
       verbose      - show full room description

Preference is per-player and is saved.

score.title: score / stats
score.summary: Show your character sheet, or examine a target.
score.body:
Usage: score                 - your full character sheet
       score <player>        - inspect another player
       score <monster>       - inspect a monster in the room
       qs                    - quick one-line status (works in combat)
       st                    - short alias for score

qs.title: qs (quick score)
qs.summary: Compact status display, usable in combat.
qs.body:
Usage: qs

Shows your name, level, XP, HP, mana, gold, equipment damage, PVP status, and cycle timer in a compact form. Safe to use mid-combat.

consider.title: consider
consider.summary: Estimate the outcome of fighting a monster.
consider.body:
Usage: consider <monster>
       con <monster>

Compares your damage and HP to the monster's and gives a verdict ranging from 'easy' to 'will crush you in moments'. Considers your equipped weapon and armor.

attack.title: attack
attack.body:
Usage: attack <monster>
       kill <monster>
       k <monster>

Starts a real-time combat round (one full exchange every ~3 seconds). Partial monster names work - 'attack drag' will hit the dragon in the room.

During combat you can: flee, cast <spell>, use <potion>, qs, look.

flee.title: flee
flee.body:
Usage: flee
       run
       escape

60% chance to escape through a random exit. Failed flees still cost you a round of monster attack. You also lose a small amount of XP and gold on success.

cast.title: cast
cast.body:
Usage: cast <spell> [target]

Use 'spells' to see what you know. Spells cost mana and have cooldowns. Most spells work in combat; recall, sanctuary, summon_player, and gate are blocked while fighting.

spells.title: spells
spells.body:
Usage: spells
       spells <school>

Lists spells across the six schools: Malefic, Theft, Divination, Combat, Protection, Utility. Spells unlock by character level.

mana.title: mana
mana.body: Mana is consumed by spells and regenerates passively over time. Mana potions and certain consumables can restore it. Maximum mana scales with level.

inventory.title: inventory
inventory.body:
Usage: inventory
       inv
       i

Shows everything in your bag (max 20 items by default), plus your gold.

equipment.title: equipment
equipment.summary: List equipped gear across all 8 slots.
equipment.body:
Usage: equipment
       eq
       gear

Slots: weapon, body armor, shield, head, neck, hands, feet, finger.
Weapons add to damage. Everything else adds to your armor rating.

equip.title: equip
equip.body:
Usage: equip <item>
       wear <item>
       wield <item>

The slot is determined by the item. Old item in that slot is moved back to your bag. You cannot change equipment while in combat.

unequip.title: unequip
unequip.body:
Usage: unequip <slot>
       remove <slot>

Valid slots: weapon, armor, shield, head, neck, hands, feet, finger.

get.title: get / take
get.summary: Pick something up off the floor.
get.body:
Usage: get <item>
       take <item>

Partial names work. 'get all' is not currently supported.

drop.title: drop
drop.summary: Drop an item from your bag.
drop.body:
Usage: drop <item>

The item is left on the floor of the current room and can be picked up again - or by someone else.

use.title: use / drink / eat
use.summary: Consume a potion, food, or scroll.
use.body:
Usage: use <item>
       drink <potion>
       eat <food>

Works in combat. Potions restore HP and/or mana.

examine.title: examine
examine.body:
Usage: examine <item>
       inspect <item>

Shows item type, level requirement, bonuses, and description.

say.title: say
say.summary: Speak to others in the same room.
say.body:
Usage: say <message>
       ' <message>

Only people in your current room hear you.

shout.title: shout
shout.body:
Usage: shout <message>
       ! <message>

All online players hear it. Use sparingly.

tell.title: tell / whisper
tell.body:
Usage: tell <player> <message>
       whisper <player> <message>

If the target is AFK you'll get an auto-reply.

emote.title: emote
emote.summary: Roleplay action visible to your room.
emote.body:
Usage: emote <action>
       : <action>

Example: ': bows deeply' will be shown as 'YourName bows deeply'.

channels.title: channels
channels.body:
Channels: newbie, ooc, gossip, trade.

Usage: <channel> <message>     - send a message
       <channel>               - toggle the channel on/off
       channels                - list channels and your subscriptions

Messages are server-wide, and only people subscribed to that channel see them.

who.title: who
who.body:
Usage: who
       players
       online

Shows everyone currently connected, their level, and AFK status.

whois.title: whois / finger
whois.body:
Usage: whois <player>
       finger <player>

Shows level, title, location, and PVP status.

afk.title: afk
afk.summary: Mark yourself as away from keyboard.
afk.body:
Usage: afk [optional message]
       back

While AFK your name shows [AFK] in who lists. People who 'tell' you get an auto-reply. Moving clears AFK automatically.

ignore.title: ignore
ignore.body:
Usage: ignore <player>
       unignore <player>
       ignore                - show your ignore list

Ignored players' tells, channel messages, and shouts are silenced for you.

alias.title: alias
alias.body:
Usage: alias <name> <command>     - create or replace an alias
       alias <name>               - show one alias
       alias                      - list all your aliases
       unalias <name>             - remove an alias

Example: alias k attack
         k goblin    -> runs 'attack goblin'

Only the first word is replaced. Aliases are saved with your character.

aliases.title: aliases
aliases.body:
Usage: aliases

Same as 'alias' with no arguments. See 'help alias' for details.

map.title: map
map.summary: Show a small ASCII map of your zone.
map.body:
Usage: map

Shows a 7x7 window centered on your room. Symbols:
  @  your current room
  *  visited room
  #  unvisited room
  (blank) no room or another zone

bestiary.title: bestiary
bestiary.body:
Usage: bestiary               - list all monsters by level
       bestiary <name>        - show details for one monster

Details include level, HP, strength, type, lore, and any guaranteed loot.

monsters.title: monsters / spawns
monsters.summary: List currently active monsters and spawn stats.
monsters.body:
Usage: monsters       - list every monster currently in the world
       spawns         - show spawn statistics by zone

quests.title: quests
quests.body:
Usage: quests
       quest
       journal
       j

Shows your active quests, objectives, and progress. Talk to NPCs to receive new quests.

accept.title: accept
accept.summary: Accept a quest from the giver.
accept.body:
Usage: accept <quest_id>

Use this when an NPC has just offered you a quest. Quest IDs are shown in the offer message.

abandon.title: abandon
abandon.body:
Usage: abandon <quest_id>

Gives up the quest and may damage your relationship with the giver. Quests reset every world cycle anyway.

save.title: save
save.body:
Usage: save

The game also auto-saves every 2 minutes, on level-up, on boss defeat, on death, and when you quit. Manual saves are useful before logging out abruptly.

quit.title: quit
quit.body:
Usage: quit

Saves your character, shows a session summary, and disconnects. Always quit cleanly.

pray.title: pray
pray.body:
Usage: pray

In a chapel room, restores you to full HP. Five-minute personal cooldown. Cannot pray during combat. Chapels are at rooms 001, 020, 050, 062, 090.

pvp.title: pvp
pvp.body:
Usage: pvp on        - become attackable (and able to attack others)
       pvp off       - peace mode (5-minute cooldown to disable after enabling)
       pvp status    - show current state
       surrender     - forfeit a PVP fight

Level difference is capped: you cannot attack anyone 5 or more levels below you.

time.title: time
time.summary: Time until the next world reset.
time.body:
Usage: time
       reset_timer

The world cycles every hour. On reset, monsters respawn, players teleport to room 001, full HP is restored, and cycle leaderboards roll over.

leaderboard.title: leaderboard
leaderboard.body:
Usage: leaderboard

Tracks XP, monsters slain, gold earned, and bosses defeated this cycle. Resets when the world cycles.

channels-on-off.title: channel toggling
channels-on-off.summary: Turn individual channels on or off.
channels-on-off.body:
Type a channel name with no message to toggle:
  newbie
  ooc
  gossip
  trade

This is also visible via the 'channels' command.

neo_kyoto.title: Neo Kyoto
neo_kyoto.summary: The second realm. A staging environment that got out of hand.
neo_kyoto.body:
Neo Kyoto is the post-remort realm: rooms 201-300, 10 zones, 5 named bosses, dense cyberpunk infrastructure run by Nomagio's automation in his absence. Entry requires remort tier 1 or higher.

Get in via the Nomagios Transit Terminal: walk to room_100 (The Perfect Sanctum) and go up. Terminal Officer Koma stamps you in. Walking Neo Kyoto without remorting is not possible - the staff door does not appear until you've rebooted at least once.

Key systems unique to Neo Kyoto: the Hack skill, Replicant/Human Affinity, the Data damage type, and the questline known as the Echo of Lyssara.

shuttle.title: shuttle
shuttle.summary: The Nomagios Transit Terminal between Eldoria and Neo Kyoto.
shuttle.body:
Two-way passage between Server 1 (Eldoria) and Server 2 (Neo Kyoto):

  room_100 (Eldoria, Perfect Sanctum) -- up --> room_201 (Neo Kyoto, Arrivals)
  room_201 -- down --> room_100

The Eldoria-side door is invisible to players with remortTier 0. After your first remort, it becomes visible and traversable. Pets, gear, and gold all carry across. Quest state is per-realm but persists across crossings.

hack.title: hack
hack.summary: Hack a Neo Kyoto terminal with skill + d20 vs DC.
hack.body:
Usage: hack                  list hackable terminals in this room
       hack <keyword>        attempt to hack the matching terminal

Mechanic: roll = 1d20 + player.skills.hack + buffs (Static Tea grants +2 for 5 min). If roll >= terminal DC, success.

Failure rolls 60% to spawn a Security Subroutine into your room.

Terminals are one-shot per cycle. Five terminals are seeded across Neo Kyoto: rooms 215, 232, 245, 267, 295 (DCs 8, 12, 10, 14, 18).

Train hack at Hiro in room_213, Neon Soi: 5g per point, capped at 10. The 'train hack' command works only when you're in his room.

affinity.title: affinity / standing
affinity.summary: Replicant / Human meter that gates dialogue and gear.
affinity.body:
Usage: affinity
       standing

Neo Kyoto tracks two non-negative integers: Replicant and Human. Quests grant deltas on turn-in:

  performance_review        -> Human +3
  dreams_of_sheep           -> Replicant +3
  orphans_in_the_machine    -> Replicant +2

Standing label:
  R >= 5 and H < 5    -> Replicant-leaning
  H >= 5 and R < 5    -> Human-leaning
  Both >= 3           -> Balanced (both paths active)
  else                -> Unaligned

Affinity persists across remort. The Replicant Vow Ring requires Replicant 5 to equip.

data_damage.title: Data damage type
data_damage.summary: The sixth damage type, native to Neo Kyoto.
data_damage.body:
Damage types: physical, fire, harmonic, shadow, cold, data.

Neo Kyoto monsters and bosses inherit a realm-wide resist profile: harmonic 75% resist, data 25% vulnerability (-25). Your Eldoria tuning forks land at quarter efficiency here. Data weapons land at 125%.

Data weapon shop runs: Rusty's Chromeshop (room_214), Tyrell-Nomagios Procurement (room_225), and the Back Of The Bazaar (room_277). Craftable Data weapons: stun_baton (L16), ice_breaker_rifle (L25), segfault_cleaver (L26). Crafting all three earns the 'Compiled' achievement.

Eldoria mobs are not vulnerable to Data, so it is not a free cross-realm upgrade. Pack a Data weapon and a non-Data sidearm.

chiyo_7.title: Chiyo-7, the Deprecated
chiyo_7.body:
Level 18, 1300 HP, 60 STR. Physical damage. Standard Neo Kyoto resists.

Signature: at 50% HP her cert expires. Combat rounds tick at 1.5x speed for the rest of the fight, but her hits deal 0 damage. The challenge is the first half. Drops Chiyo-7's Expired Cert.

account_manager.title: The Account Manager
account_manager.body:
Level 20, 1600 HP, 70 STR. Physical damage. Standard Neo Kyoto resists.

Signature: every 3 rounds escalates - spawns a Junior Associate add. Her damage scales +10% per live add. Kill adds first; she compounds otherwise. Drops Managerial Letter of Recommendation.

babel_fish_regent.title: Babel Fish Regent
babel_fish_regent.body:
Level 24, 2200 HP, 90 STR. Data damage type. Resists rotate every round.

Signature: each round flips an additional resisted damage type at random and announces it. Bring a multi-type kit - a Data primary plus a physical or harmonic sidearm covers most flips. Drops Babel Fish Crown.

deep_pool.title: The Deep Pool
deep_pool.body:
Level 26, 2500 HP, 95 STR. Data damage type. Standard Neo Kyoto resists.

Signature: drains 25-44 mana per swing instead of dealing HP damage. Once your mana hits zero, attacks bleed through at +50% damage. Stockpile Static Tea, Patch Notes Scroll, Admin Cola before engaging. Drops Deep-Pool Pearl.

sysadmin_exe.title: SYSADMIN.EXE
sysadmin_exe.body:
Level 30, 3500 HP, 120 STR. Data damage type. Standard Neo Kyoto resists.

Phase 1 (100->66% HP): stock combat.
Phase 2 (66->33% HP): autoscaler engages. Spawns a cron_daemon add immediately and another every 10 seconds.
Phase 3 (<33% HP): pages oncall. Your next 2 spell casts have a 50% chance of failing with a 503 error.

Group play strongly recommended. On death, Nomagio's distress transmission from Server 3 plays. Drops SYSADMIN.EXE's Root Key.

neon_soi.title: Neon Soi
neon_soi.body:
Rain-slicked alleys, noodle stalls, replicant buskers, street hackers. Spawn rate 70%. Mobs: neon_yakuza, rogue_replicant, bazaar_cutpurse, noodle_vendor_aggro. Boss at room_220 (Chiyo-7).

Key NPCs: Hiro (room_213, hack-skill trainer). Shop: Rusty's Chromeshop (room_214).

the_stack.title: The Stack
the_stack.summary: Server-core zone (rooms 231-240). Hack skill shines here.
the_stack.body:
Literal datacentre interior. Humming coolant aisles, blinking racks. Spawn rate 75%. Mobs: data_ghost, security_subroutine, memory_leak_wraith. No zone boss - mid-realm hub.

Hack terminal: rack maintenance panel in room_232 (DC 12), drops Bytecode Shard + Deleted Memory.

Easter egg: the Apprentice's Tuning Fork (signed L.P.) lies on the floor of room_237. The Echo of Lyssara wanders this room, asking for it.

replicant_quarter.title: Replicant Quarter
replicant_quarter.summary: Sanctuary zone (rooms 281-290). Affinity choices peak here.
replicant_quarter.body:
Quiet philosophical district. Spawn rate 60%. Mostly passive mobs: synthetic_orphan, philosopher_model, rogue_replicant. Combat encounter at room_290 (Mother of Orphans, dialogue-resolvable at Replicant 5+).

Key NPC: Wren (room_283), philosopher-model. The largest sharded fragment of Lyssara's consciousness, though Wren does not yet know it.

midnight_market.title: Midnight Market
midnight_market.body:
Grey-market and black-market stalls. Spawn rate 55%. Mobs: deprecated_bladerunner, philosopher_model, bazaar_cutpurse.

Key NPC: Barkeep 42 (room_273). Owns three quests: the_cold_aisle, babel_fish_problem, paging_oncall (the realm capstone). Shop: The Back Of The Bazaar (room_277) - best sell-back rate (0.6x) and the only single-source for crafting reagents.

lyssara.title: The Echo of Lyssara
lyssara.summary: Spectral easter-egg encounter in The Stack (room_237).
lyssara.body:
Lyssara the Patient was an Eldoria archmage and predecessor to Thessarian on the seat of Logic. Eight years before the player arrives, she crossed the staging-environment shuttle to debug what she believed to be a memory leak. SYSADMIN.EXE killed her to prevent the fix; her consciousness sharded into the Replicant Quarter's philosopher-models. What walks room_237 is the half-rendered echo: the part of her that was working the problem when she died.

She does not know she is dead. She speaks in present tense. She is asking for her tuning fork.
